title.en: Your First Shabbat: A Step-by-Step Guide for Complete Beginners
title.zh: 你的第一个安息日：完全初学者的分步指南
title.he: השבת הראשונה שלך: מדריך שלב אחר שלב למתחילים מוחלטים

description: Everything you need to know to observe your first Shabbat, from Friday afternoon preparation to Havdalah Saturday night.
target_audience: Complete beginners to Judaism and Shabbat observance
cultural_focus: Adapted for readers living in China and Asia with limited access to Jewish communities
author: Torah Light Editorial Team

historical_periods_referenced.chinese_context: Jewish merchants observing Shabbat along Silk Road
Kaifeng Jewish community establishment and Shabbat traditions
Continued Shabbat observance despite assimilation pressures

practical_elements.timeline: 25-hour observance from Friday sunset to Saturday night

accessibility_notes.dietary_substitutions: Grape juice for wine, any bread for challah in emergencies
accessibility_notes.equipment_alternatives: Regular candles for Shabbat candles, creative solutions for missing items
accessibility_notes.solo_practitioner_guidance: Specific instructions for those observing alone

cultural_adaptations.chinese_context: References to Chinese historical periods and cultural parallels
cultural_adaptations.asia_specific_guidance: Advice for those living far from Jewish communities
cultural_adaptations.universal_themes: Family gathering, gratitude, rest, and spiritual reflection
